name: Cailleach
description: The Cailleach (also known as the Cailleach Bhéara, the Hag of Beara, or the Cailleach Mhor) is a divine hag figure in Scottish and Irish mythology. She is associated with winter, storms, and the creation of landscapes. Often depicted as an old woman with immense power and wisdom, the Cailleach is a complex figure, simultaneously representing destruction and renewal. She is believed to control the weather, particularly winter storms, and is often associated with mountains and bodies of water. In some traditions, she is seen as a creator goddess, responsible for shaping the land with her hammer or staff, while in others she is a harbinger of death and decay. The Cailleach is also linked to the cycles of the seasons, representing the transition from autumn to winter and the dormancy of the earth. She is a powerful and enduring symbol of the wild, untamed forces of nature, embodying both the harshness and beauty of the natural world. Her legends often feature themes of transformation, power, and the cyclical nature of life and death. She is a formidable figure, respected and feared in equal measure, and remains a significant presence in Celtic folklore.
subtitle: Winter Hag, Shaper of the Land
appearance: The Cailleach is typically depicted as an old woman with a gaunt face, wild white hair, and piercing eyes. She often wears ragged clothes and carries a staff or hammer, which she uses to shape the land. Her appearance can vary depending on the region and the specific myth, but she is always portrayed as an imposing and powerful figure.
habitat: Mountains, desolate landscapes, remote islands, and areas associated with harsh weather conditions.
behavior: The Cailleach is a solitary figure, often dwelling in remote and desolate places. She is known for her unpredictable temper and her control over the weather. She can be both benevolent and malevolent, depending on her mood and the circumstances. She is often associated with guarding sacred places and testing those who dare to enter her domain.
weaknesses: Spring (Her power wanes with the arrival of spring)
Youth (The loss of her aged form)

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Loch Ness
keyMyths.description: The Cailleach is said to have created Loch Ness by carelessly washing her soiled apron, causing the water to overflow and form the loch. This myth highlights her connection to the landscape and the power of nature.

keyMyths.title: The Cailleach's Winter Reign
keyMyths.description: Each year, the Cailleach ushers in winter, ruling over the land with her storms and icy grip. As spring approaches, she either transforms into a young woman or is banished, allowing the land to thaw.

sources.author: Patricia Monaghan
